records.why_it_matters: NTM isolation persisted mostly outside ETI exposure, underscoring that adult microbiology surveillance remains important despite highly effective modulators.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract reports cohort size, ETI exposure timing, microbiology patterns, and a clear surveillance conclusion.

secondary_themes: Care burden, adherence, and psychosocial impact
Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: Patient-focused target product profiles could help align phage therapy development with practical CF infection treatment needs and adoption barriers.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract describes patient, clinician, innovator, and regulatory input plus specific TPP outputs.

secondary_themes: Emerging genetic, RNA, and cell-model therapies
records.why_it_matters: An antivirulence compound reduced Stenotrophomonas biofilm and pathogenicity signals, suggesting adjunctive options for difficult MDR CF infections.
records.confidence_reason: Structured preclinical work includes antimicrobial, biofilm, gene expression, cell, and larval infection assays.

records.why_it_matters: Comparing CF and non-CF bronchiectasis Pseudomonas phenotypes informs whether combined antimicrobial trial populations are biologically defensible.
records.confidence_reason: Unstructured abstract still provides methods, strain counts, resistance differences, and cautious trial-design interpretation.

records.why_it_matters: A rapid saliva LAMP assay may help preserve Pseudomonas monitoring as sputum becomes less available in the modulator era.
records.confidence_reason: Structured assay-development abstract gives target gene, workflow, spiked-saliva performance, and next validation step.

secondary_themes: Precision CFTR biology and rare variants
records.why_it_matters: Linking CFTR-driven sulfur metabolism to Staphylococcus adaptation and Pseudomonas persistence identifies a mechanistic driver of pathogen succession.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract combines sputum metabolomics, isolate genomics, cellular models, and mouse validation.

secondary_themes: Emerging genetic, RNA, and cell-model therapies
records.why_it_matters: Nanohydrogel delivery that improves biofilm penetration and antibiotic synergy could help address resistant CF airway pathogens.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract reports nanocarrier characterization, MDR isolate testing, MIC reduction, and biofilm effects.

records.why_it_matters: Falling sputum sampling and culture positivity after ETI raise practical questions about how to maintain reliable pathogen surveillance.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract includes a decade of samples, adult-center cohort size, pre/post ETI comparisons, and sampling concordance findings.

records.why_it_matters: Reduced stool resistance-gene abundance after ETI suggests indirect antimicrobial-resistance benefits may follow lower IV antibiotic exposure.
records.confidence_reason: Structured paired-stool metagenomic analysis reports sample size, ETI timing, ARG change, and antibiotic-use reduction.

records.why_it_matters: Registry trends separating pandemic and ETI periods help attribute chronic infection declines to sustained modulator exposure rather than COVID-era behavior changes.
records.confidence_reason: Structured Norwegian registry analysis compares 2019, 2021, and 2024 with longitudinal and cross-sectional infection models.

records.why_it_matters: Real-world cultures show ETI-associated reductions in Pseudomonas and Staphylococcus but a relative rise in H. influenzae, implying airway ecology is shifting rather than simply resolving.
records.confidence_reason: Structured retrospective culture comparison includes large pre/post ETI culture counts and organism-specific results.

records.why_it_matters: Newer antipseudomonal beta-lactams appeared tolerable and inflammation-improving in advanced CF disease with MDR exacerbations, supporting selective use in refractory cases.
records.confidence_reason: Structured retrospective review reports advanced disease context, treatment encounters, CRP response, lung function heterogeneity, and tolerability.

secondary_themes: Precision CFTR biology and rare variants
records.why_it_matters: Genomic and phenotypic analysis of respiratory E. coli may clarify why a non-classical CF organism can persist chronically in some lungs.
records.confidence_reason: Unstructured summary gives isolate numbers and methods but the available text provides limited result detail.

records.why_it_matters: ABPA biomarker trends may support diagnosis but appear limited for early detection and monitoring, emphasizing the need for broader diagnostic frameworks.
records.confidence_reason: Structured pediatric retrospective analysis reports biomarker correlations, case capture, and limitations after diagnosis.

records.why_it_matters: Mouse data suggest vaping can induce CF-relevant gut-lung microbial and metabolic remodeling, supporting attention to environmental modifiers of infection risk.
records.confidence_reason: Structured animal study reports exposure groups, microbiome findings, metabolomics, and a clear mechanistic interpretation.

records.why_it_matters: Pilot detection of Pneumocystis colonization raises questions about fungal surveillance and risk assessment, especially before transplantation.
records.confidence_reason: Structured pilot has a small cohort and hypothesis-generating associations without significant between-group differences.

records.why_it_matters: Long-term Russian registry microbiology trends provide population-level context for pathogen shifts and possible targeted-therapy effects.
records.confidence_reason: Registry summary reports multi-year organism prevalence but has limited methods detail and a likely date-range typo.

secondary_themes: Precision CFTR biology and rare variants
records.why_it_matters: Within-patient strain variation changes predicted bacterial interactions, cautioning against conclusions based on single representative isolates.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract describes isolate collection, interaction assays, lineage-specific results, and implications for prediction.

secondary_themes: Precision CFTR biology and rare variants
records.why_it_matters: Strain-level microbiome dynamics may explain persistent colonization, resistance evolution, and why community context matters for antimicrobial response.
records.confidence_reason: Unstructured abstract provides strong rationale and methods direction but limited visible results in the available summary.

secondary_themes: Emerging genetic, RNA, and cell-model therapies
records.why_it_matters: Validated donor-derived CF gut models create a platform to test drug-microbiome interactions that remain unresolved despite modulators.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract reports donor numbers, longitudinal model stability, microbiome differences, and multi-omic validation.

records.why_it_matters: Prospective home viral sampling suggests respiratory viruses may explain a meaningful share of adult CF pulmonary exacerbations.
records.confidence_reason: Preliminary unstructured study reports sampling numbers and positivity patterns but follow-up is still early.

records.why_it_matters: Patient-derived and in vitro findings support more nuanced management of viral-bacterial coinfections in young children with CF exacerbations.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract includes pediatric longitudinal sampling, viral PCR, microbiome, immune markers, and mechanistic assays.

records.primary_theme: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
secondary_themes: Care burden, adherence, and psychosocial impact
records.why_it_matters: SES-linked differences in knowledge and possible MRSA colonization point to infection-risk pathways beyond education alone.
records.confidence_reason: Structured cross-sectional pediatric analysis reports SES construction, knowledge scores, colonization trends, and limitations.

records.why_it_matters: Large pediatric isolate surveillance highlights continuing resistance concerns, including colistin-resistant Pseudomonas and rising staphylococcal resistance.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract reports isolate counts, organism distribution, susceptibility testing, and resistance findings.

records.why_it_matters: Virus detection was much more common during exacerbations in preschool children, supporting viral testing in early CF exacerbation assessment.
records.confidence_reason: Structured prospective pediatric study reports cohort size, PCR strategy, odds of viral detection during PEx, and virus types.

records.why_it_matters: A harmonized pediatric-adult cardiovascular cohort can define when CF care should begin monitoring emerging atherosclerotic risk.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract is a study design with ongoing enrollment and no outcome data yet.

secondary_themes: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
Care burden, adherence, and psychosocial impact
records.why_it_matters: Patient and care-team surveys show digestive symptoms remain common on modulators and expose gaps in specialist gastroenterology access.
records.confidence_reason: Structured project includes large patient survey, national care-team input, and multidisciplinary priority setting.

records.why_it_matters: ETI-associated lipid changes may shift adult CF care toward cardiovascular risk monitoring and dietary counseling.
records.confidence_reason: Structured adult pre/post ETI study reports lipid and BMI changes with clear metabolic interpretation.

records.why_it_matters: Exploring links among steatosis, body composition, and glucose markers may refine cardiometabolic risk assessment in CF liver disease.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract reports eligibility, elastography, body composition, glucose testing, and regression findings.

records.why_it_matters: Esophageal inflammatory markers linked to GI symptom scores suggest upper-GI inflammation may persist even during ETI therapy.
records.confidence_reason: Structured cross-sectional study reports age range, ETI groups, biomarker panel, PedsQL correlations, and limitations.

records.why_it_matters: Weak PTH-vitamin D correlation questions whether raised PTH should be treated as a straightforward marker of inadequate vitamin D replacement in CF.
records.confidence_reason: Structured adult annual-review analysis reports sample counts, paired labs, pancreatic status associations, and assay concerns.

records.why_it_matters: Prospective pediatric registry screening suggests autoimmune comorbidities and islet autoantibodies may be more common than expected in CF.
records.confidence_reason: Structured registry abstract reports screening definitions, cohort size, and prevalence for celiac, thyroid, and islet autoimmunity.

records.why_it_matters: Pks-positive E. coli may become a risk biomarker or intervention target for early colorectal neoplasia in adults with CF.
records.confidence_reason: Structured SCREEN-CF analysis reports colonoscopy groups, pks testing, odds ratio, and biomarker interpretation.

records.why_it_matters: High pediatric intestinal inflammation with reduced calprotectin after ETI supports monitoring GI inflammation as part of modulator-era care.
records.confidence_reason: Structured pediatric study reports symptoms, fecal calprotectin, pre/post ETI results, and symptom persistence.

records.why_it_matters: Routine liver and kidney labs after ETI were mostly stable except bilirubin, informing safety monitoring in clinical practice.
records.confidence_reason: Structured single-center pre/post study reports monitored labs, cohort characteristics, and statistical findings.

records.why_it_matters: Improved HDL efflux after ETI suggests CFTR correction may alter cardiovascular biology, not just conventional lipid levels.
records.confidence_reason: Structured longitudinal adult serum study reports cell-based cholesterol efflux/loading assays before and after ETI.

records.why_it_matters: CFAbd-Score captured high GI symptom prevalence and links with abdominal pain, supporting routine symptom measurement in pediatric care.
records.confidence_reason: Structured cross-sectional pediatric study reports questionnaire use, cohort size, symptom prevalence, and correlations.

records.why_it_matters: Metformin may have a role in selected adult CFRD management, but evidence remains limited by sample size and non-randomized design.
records.confidence_reason: Structured retrospective study reports clinical metrics before and after metformin, with small sample and mostly non-significant tests.

records.why_it_matters: Vitamin A and E shifts after pediatric modulator therapy reinforce the need for active fat-soluble vitamin monitoring and dose adjustment.
records.confidence_reason: Structured single-center pediatric pre/post study reports vitamin levels, statistics, and adherence interpretation.

secondary_themes: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: Colonoscopy screening decisions must account for advanced lung disease, comorbidities, and patient priorities, with alternatives needed for unsuitable patients.
records.confidence_reason: Structured service review reports eligibility, reasons for unsuitability or refusal, uptake, and screening implications.

records.primary_theme: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: CF-tailored bowel preparation protocols may improve colonoscopy quality and reduce missed lesions or repeat procedures.
records.confidence_reason: Structured multicenter retrospective cohort reports preparation quality and pre/post protocol effects.

records.primary_theme: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
secondary_themes: Care burden, adherence, and psychosocial impact
records.why_it_matters: A specialist CF gastroenterology clinic addresses complex symptom referrals and highlights the value of embedded GI expertise in adult CF services.
records.confidence_reason: Structured clinic review reports appointment activity, referral indications, investigations, diagnoses, and future satisfaction assessment.

records.why_it_matters: Improved liver stiffness after two years of ETI suggests some pediatric CF liver changes may be modifiable, though severe cases still need close monitoring.
records.confidence_reason: Structured pediatric pre/post elastography study reports METAVIR shifts, kPa changes, and a cirrhosis case caveat.

records.why_it_matters: Rising high vitamin A and E levels during ETI show supplementation protocols may need reassessment to avoid over-replacement.
records.confidence_reason: Structured pediatric study reports 117 children, HPLC/ELISA measurements, pre/post vitamin changes, and safety concern.

secondary_themes: Precision CFTR biology and rare variants
records.why_it_matters: Residual-function genotype does not necessarily protect against low bone density, so skeletal monitoring should not be relaxed based on pancreatic sufficiency or BMI.
records.confidence_reason: Structured adult DEXA analysis reports genotype strata, BMD differences, and clinical interpretation.

secondary_themes: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: An HbA1c-first pathway may reduce CFRD screening burden if CGM is targeted to those most likely to need treatment.
records.confidence_reason: Structured service evaluation reports screened adults, CGM-diagnosed CFRD, HbA1c strata, and treatment implications.

records.why_it_matters: Declines in fecal inflammatory markers during ETI suggest early modulator treatment may reduce intestinal inflammation in young children.
records.confidence_reason: Structured paired pediatric biomarker study reports calprotectin, neopterin, myeloperoxidase, and significance by marker.

records.primary_theme: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
secondary_themes: Care burden, adherence, and psychosocial impact
records.why_it_matters: Structured CFRD education may improve glycemic control for some adults while providing a scalable education model for CF diabetes services.
records.confidence_reason: Structured programme evaluation reports participation, HbA1c direction, delivery format, and rollout implications.

records.why_it_matters: Longitudinal pre/post ETI cardiometabolic trajectories suggest baseline characteristics shape post-modulator metabolic risk and monitoring needs.
records.confidence_reason: Structured cohort analysis reports three-year pre/post data, mixed models, and predictors of HbA1c and fructosamine slopes.

records.why_it_matters: A subset, especially younger patients, may recover or fluctuate in pancreatic function on modulators, supporting regular fecal elastase reassessment.
records.confidence_reason: Structured single-center analysis reports 2203 fecal elastase values, 132 patients, pancreatic status categories, and enzyme implications.

records.why_it_matters: Multicenter administrative data suggest pediatric constipation and obstruction encounters declined after HEMT, extending modulator benefits beyond lungs.
records.confidence_reason: Structured PHIS database study reports era definitions, encounter counts, and statistically significant declines.

records.primary_theme: Precision CFTR biology and rare variants
records.why_it_matters: Protein-level CFTR genotype classification may better identify patients at risk for hepatobiliary involvement than traditional variant classes.
records.confidence_reason: Structured genotype-phenotype study reports 457 patients, liver involvement rates, reclassification approach, and significant associations.

records.why_it_matters: Prolonged neonatal jaundice was less common in CF than controls, arguing against treating it as a typical early CF sign while still evaluating cholestatic cases.
records.confidence_reason: Structured retrospective cohort compares CF and healthy children with jaundice duration and clinical interpretation.

records.why_it_matters: Some children reduced or stopped PERT after modulators, showing that clinical enzyme needs may change even when fecal elastase remains low.
records.confidence_reason: Structured pediatric retrospective review reports modulator exposure, pancreatic status conversions, PERT dose changes, and FE findings.

records.primary_theme: Care burden, adherence, and psychosocial impact
secondary_themes: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: Patient input favors brief, CF-specific, on-demand diabetes resources as adjuncts to individualized care rather than replacements.
records.confidence_reason: Structured focus-group abstract reports participant characteristics, themes, preferred formats, and co-design implications.

records.primary_theme: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: Post-lung-transplant CF patients need tailored bowel preparation because inadequate prep remains common despite intensive regimens.
records.confidence_reason: Structured retrospective screening-protocol evaluation reports post-transplant colonoscopy counts, BBPS scores, and repeat-procedure rates.

records.why_it_matters: High adenoma and polyp burden after lung transplant supports early, intensive, ongoing colonoscopic surveillance in CF transplant recipients.
records.confidence_reason: Structured retrospective colonoscopy review reports eligible patients, polyp detection, histology, and comparison with CF Foundation data.

records.why_it_matters: HbA1c-first CFRD screening could miss many CGM-diagnosed cases, highlighting tradeoffs between reduced burden and early detection.
records.confidence_reason: Structured retrospective adult study reports diagnostic pathway comparison, HbA1c strata, medication initiation, and complications.

secondary_themes: Care burden, adherence, and psychosocial impact
Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: Hybrid closed-loop insulin systems may change CFRD management patterns and engagement, but larger studies are needed for sustained clinical impact.
records.confidence_reason: Structured adult pre/post HCL analysis reports device transition, glycemic metrics, insulin dosing, and follow-up duration.

records.primary_theme: Care burden, adherence, and psychosocial impact
secondary_themes: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: High satisfaction with hybrid closed-loop therapy suggests diabetes technology may reduce perceived CFRD burden when multidisciplinary support is available.
records.confidence_reason: Structured survey reports response rate, satisfaction, perceived burden, recommendations, and support themes.

records.why_it_matters: C-peptide may help stratify insulin deficiency and tailor CFRD treatment, complementing glucose metrics.
records.confidence_reason: Structured adult cohort reports C-peptide thresholds, treatment groups, odds of insulin use, and HbA1c association.

records.why_it_matters: Femoral bone density loss persisted after ETI, supporting continued bone surveillance even as other CF outcomes improve.
records.confidence_reason: Structured longitudinal adult DEXA study reports multiple timepoints, post-ETI trajectories, and risk-factor modeling.

records.primary_theme: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: Embedding elastography into annual adult CF assessment could improve practical liver-disease surveillance beyond routine labs and ultrasound.
records.confidence_reason: Structured service implementation abstract reports training, scan workflow, early results, and monitoring rationale.

secondary_themes: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: Bowel cancer screening audits can reveal missed eligible patients and documentation gaps that are fixable through review workflows.
records.confidence_reason: Structured audit reports eligible population, offer and consent rates, barriers, and process changes.

records.why_it_matters: A rare precocious puberty case flags uncertainty about pubertal development patterns as nutrition and modulator exposure change in CF.
records.confidence_reason: Evidence is a single case report with explicit statement that causal inference is not possible.

secondary_themes: Emerging genetic, RNA, and cell-model therapies
records.why_it_matters: Clinoptilolite therapy may reduce GI symptom burden in selected adults, but prospective controlled evidence is still needed.
records.confidence_reason: Structured retrospective observational study reports CFAbd-Score response but lacks a control group and has small sample size.

records.why_it_matters: Three-year pediatric data suggest modulators improve liver biochemistry but may not reverse liver stiffness, refining expectations for hepatobiliary monitoring.
records.confidence_reason: Structured prospective single-center study reports biomarkers and elastography before and after 36 months.

records.why_it_matters: Matched pediatric registry data did not show clear glucose-metabolism benefit from modulators, cautioning against assuming CFRD risk is reduced.
records.confidence_reason: Structured matched cohort analysis reports treated and control groups, OGTT data availability, and null metabolic findings.

records.why_it_matters: Persistent vitamin D insufficiency despite supplementation suggests dosing and pancreatic-status assumptions may need revision in children.
records.confidence_reason: Structured repeated-measures pediatric analysis reports sample size, GEE model, determinants, and estimated dose implications.

records.primary_theme: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
secondary_themes: Care burden, adherence, and psychosocial impact
records.why_it_matters: Online structured CFRD education may improve confidence, knowledge, and access to self-management support across adult services.
records.confidence_reason: Structured rollout evaluation reports session content, feedback, confidence changes, and HbA1c comparison plan.

records.why_it_matters: Hypercalciuria prevalence on ETI appeared low, but asymptomatic stones support continued renal-metabolic monitoring as CF patients age.
records.confidence_reason: Structured cross-sectional study reports prevalence and imaging findings, but population spans children and adults and clinical impact is limited.

secondary_themes: Precision CFTR biology and rare variants
Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: High Pseudo-Bartter frequency in North Caucasus children appears driven more by climate and care practices than CFTR variant, informing prevention counseling.
records.confidence_reason: Structured regional pediatric analysis reports 160 patients, republic-level frequencies, genotype tests, and environmental interpretation.

records.why_it_matters: ETI showed a favorable liver safety profile over two years, supporting routine but not alarmist hepatobiliary monitoring.
records.confidence_reason: Structured retrospective study reports 73 patients, serial labs, non-invasive fibrosis indices, FibroScan subset, and conclusion.

records.why_it_matters: Multicenter DXA data found no significant BMD change after ETI, showing bone benefits may require longer follow-up or targeted interventions.
records.confidence_reason: Structured RECOVER cohort analysis reports paired adult and child DXA data, follow-up intervals, and biochemical findings.

records.why_it_matters: Comparing CFAbd-Score across CF, IBD, and controls contextualizes CF GI symptom burden and possible improvement during ETI.
records.confidence_reason: Structured COMET-CF-IBD abstract reports groups, CFAbd-Score domains, ETI subgroup, and statistical comparisons.

records.why_it_matters: Reduced bone density in adolescents and young adults supports ongoing bone-health monitoring even when associations with activity or nutrition are unclear.
records.confidence_reason: Structured observational study reports DXA and activity/nutrition measures but small sample limits correlation findings.

records.why_it_matters: Longitudinal CT suggests ETI changes the trajectory of pancreatic involution, adding imaging evidence to pancreatic-function questions.
records.confidence_reason: Structured imaging study reports 196 patients, 774 CT scans, segmentation methods, and significant slope changes.

records.why_it_matters: Pancreatic status can change in young children, especially after early HEMT, supporting routine fecal elastase monitoring in pediatric networks.
records.confidence_reason: Structured pediatric network audit reports 282 patients, direction of status changes, and age/timing patterns.

records.primary_theme: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: Risk-stratified liver ultrasound surveillance could reduce unnecessary imaging while preserving detection of higher-risk pediatric liver disease.
records.confidence_reason: Structured audit reports national versus local standards, ultrasound findings, missed scans, and consultant recommendations.

records.why_it_matters: Variant-specific therapy may improve pediatric glucose patterns, but all children still require metabolic monitoring regardless of therapy status.
records.confidence_reason: Structured comparative study reports OGTT patterns in only 20 children and notes continued follow-up is needed.

records.why_it_matters: Two-year pediatric growth and body-composition improvements after ETI highlight changing nutritional needs for children starting modulators early.
records.confidence_reason: Structured pre/post pediatric study reports serial anthropometry, body composition, lung function, and correlations.

secondary_themes: Care burden, adherence, and psychosocial impact
Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: A feasibility RCT will test whether intentional weight loss interventions are acceptable and safe for adults with CF as overweight becomes common.
records.confidence_reason: Structured protocol describes trial design, intervention, and approvals but has no outcome data yet.

secondary_themes: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: A CF-specific nutrition screening questionnaire may identify risk missed by BMI alone, enabling earlier dietetic intervention.
records.confidence_reason: Structured implementation abstract reports questionnaire domains, scoring, patient classification, and high-risk example.

records.why_it_matters: UK registry trends show adult CF nutrition is shifting from underweight toward overweight and obesity, changing the risk-benefit frame for BMI targets.
records.confidence_reason: Structured registry analysis reports 8,869 adults, 82,604 annual reviews, BMI trends, and lung-function associations.

records.why_it_matters: Early ETI improved BMI and pancreatic elastase particularly in children without prior LUM/IVA, supporting early pediatric initiation.
records.confidence_reason: Structured retrospective pediatric study reports cohort, subgroup analyses, BMI percentile, and elastase changes.

records.why_it_matters: North Macedonian real-world data show rapid BMI gains and reduced undernutrition after one year of ETI, supporting nutritional reassessment after initiation.
records.confidence_reason: Structured retrospective cohort reports 54 patients, repeated BMI timepoints, category shifts, and pediatric subgroup improvement.

records.why_it_matters: Diet-quality patterns in Brazilian adolescents link fruit, vegetables, and meat intake with BMI and lung-function measures, supporting dietary counseling beyond calories.
records.confidence_reason: Structured adolescent study reports questionnaire methods, regression models, PCA approach, and dietary associations.

records.why_it_matters: Osteopenia can be present despite generally adequate nutrition, supporting preventive bone-health care in pediatric CF follow-up.
records.confidence_reason: Structured cross-sectional pediatric study reports nutrition status, bone ultrasound results, and FEV1 association.

records.why_it_matters: Triple modulator therapy changed adult body composition, implying that post-modulator nutrition care should track fat and lean mass rather than BMI alone.
records.confidence_reason: Structured observational study reports significant FMI/FFMI changes but limited detail on magnitude and omitted lifestyle factors.

secondary_themes: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: Consensus research gaps show pediatric ETI nutrition guidance is lagging behind clinical rollout, especially around BMI targets and pancreatic reassessment.
records.confidence_reason: Structured systematic review and Delphi process reports panel composition, domains, and prioritized consensus statements.

records.why_it_matters: Common muscle impairment despite normal BMI and lung function supports routine body composition and strength assessment plus targeted exercise-nutrition interventions.
records.confidence_reason: Structured retrospective adult review reports body composition, strength, CPET measures, and significant associations.

records.primary_theme: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: Dietitian supplementary prescribing may streamline CF nutrition-related medication management, particularly during PERT supply constraints.
records.confidence_reason: Structured service evaluation reports prescribing episodes, medication classes, consent/CMP process, and absence of recorded errors.

records.why_it_matters: Real-world pediatric data show pancreatic recovery can occur years after modulator start, while partial recovery may create vitamin excess risk.
records.confidence_reason: Structured pediatric review reports baseline PI cohort, fecal elastase categories, sustained sufficiency, and vitamin dose reductions.

records.why_it_matters: Starting lumacaftor/ivacaftor around age one may preserve or restore pancreatic function before ETI eligibility in some children.
records.confidence_reason: Structured pediatric network review reports early Orkambi initiation, fecal elastase testing, pancreatic sufficiency, and PERT discontinuation.

records.primary_theme: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: A virtual vitamin-club model streamlined pediatric fat-soluble vitamin follow-up and dose changes during widespread modulator use.
records.confidence_reason: Structured service evaluation reports children reviewed, completeness of results, intervention frequency, and medication changes.

records.why_it_matters: In preschool children, ETI did not significantly change body composition or intake at 12 months but did alter PERT-to-fat matching.
records.confidence_reason: Structured prospective pilot reports 17 children, paired diet/body composition/enzyme variables, and a significant LU-per-fat change.

records.primary_theme: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: Marked nutritional compromise in Moldova links delayed diagnosis and limited therapy access to worse growth, emphasizing neonatal screening and comprehensive care needs.
records.confidence_reason: Structured national-center analysis reports follow-up cohort, delayed diagnosis, anthropometric deficits, and resource-limited context.

secondary_themes: Care burden, adherence, and psychosocial impact
records.why_it_matters: Hand-grip testing may be an acceptable way to start practical conversations about strength, activity, nutrition, and weight management in adult CF clinics.
records.confidence_reason: Structured feasibility project reports only nine adults and no clear correlations, but strong acceptability findings.

records.primary_theme: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: A first-1000-days nutrition QI program after early diagnosis improved growth percentiles, reinforcing intensive early dietitian follow-up.
records.confidence_reason: Structured QI abstract reports inclusion criteria, monitoring process, six-year cohort, and two-year growth percentile changes.

records.why_it_matters: Children stopping PERT after CFTR modulators need monitoring that balances fecal elastase results with constipation, symptoms, vitamins, and growth.
records.confidence_reason: Structured cohort review reports 160 children, PERT discontinuation cases, FE status, symptoms, and monitoring recommendations.

records.why_it_matters: Targeted therapy improved BMI and HbA1c but raised cholesterol measures, supporting broader metabolic surveillance in treated children.
records.confidence_reason: Structured comparative pediatric study reports therapy groups, metabolic markers, lipid changes, and monitoring conclusion.

records.why_it_matters: DHA deficiency was uncommon but associated with hepatobiliary involvement, suggesting targeted fatty-acid research may be most relevant in specific subgroups.
records.confidence_reason: Structured prospective pediatric study reports EFA levels, pancreatic and hepatobiliary status, and significant DHA-CFHBI association.

records.primary_theme: Care burden, adherence, and psychosocial impact
records.why_it_matters: Developmental screening found delays linked to maternal education, daycare, and vitamin D, supporting early multidisciplinary monitoring beyond lung and GI outcomes.
records.confidence_reason: Structured pediatric chart review reports ASQ domains, clinical predictors, regression findings, and intervention implications.
